title: Fix-first 2/3: test_model_guard.mjs is RED (18 failures) — rotted vendored-lib list
action: hooks/test_model_guard.mjs exits 1 with 18 FAILURE(S) at HEAD. Cause: copyLib() at :40-46 hand-enumerates state.mjs and fsutil.mjs, but state.mjs:5,8-9 now imports claims.mjs and reservations.mjs; the import throws inside the fixture, bee-model-guard.mjs:147-150 fail-opens (exit 0), and every expect-deny row lands 'allowed'. (1) Derive the vendored set with readdirSync over the real lib directory, exactly as hooks/test_write_guard.mjs:42-57 and hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs:162-169 already do. (2) Prove falsifiability ONCE: temporarily break hooks/bee-model-guard.mjs, confirm the suite goes red, then RESTORE IT BYTE-FOR-BYTE — git diff on the guard MUST be empty at cap. The guard is in this cell's files list ONLY to make that break-and-restore legal; no behavioral change to the guard belongs here (that is Slice 2). CORRECTION you must not repeat: an earlier draft of this plan claimed the suite 'reads as green'. It does not — it prints 18 FAILURE(S) and exits 1. The defect is that nothing RUNS it. The expect-deny rows therefore ALREADY act as sentinels under universal fail-open; do not add a new sentinel row and report it as a discovery.

truths: node hooks/test_model_guard.mjs exits 0 with zero failures
the vendored lib set is derived from the real lib directory, never a hand-kept name list
git diff on hooks/bee-model-guard.mjs is empty at cap — the falsifiability break was fully restored
the falsifiability check was actually run, and the report records what the deliberately-broken guard printed
key_links: Correct pattern already in repo: hooks/test_write_guard.mjs:42-57
prohibitions: Do not leave any behavioral change in hooks/bee-model-guard.mjs — it is listed only so the break-and-restore is legal, and git diff on it must be empty at cap
Do not edit .bee/config.json commands.verify — that is ao-fix-3
Do not weaken or delete an assertion to reach green

verify: node hooks/test_model_guard.mjs && node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs && node hooks/test_write_guard.mjs && git diff --quiet hooks/bee-model-guard.mjs

trace.outcome: test_model_guard.mjs copyLib() hand-enumerated [state.mjs, fsutil.mjs]; state.mjs now imports claims.mjs+reservations.mjs too, so the fixture's state.mjs threw ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND at import, bee-model-guard.mjs's fail-open catch (147-150) turned that into exit 0, and every expect-deny row read as allowed (18 FAILURE(S)). Fixed by deriving the vendored set with fs.readdirSync over the real lib dir, mirroring hooks/test_write_guard.mjs:42-57 exactly. Falsifiability proven: temporarily changed the guard's deny return from 2 to 0, reran the suite (6 FAILURE(S) on exactly the expect-deny rows: row1, row-table[Agent], row-table[Task], row20c), then restored the guard byte-for-byte (git diff --quiet passes). No behavioral change shipped in bee-model-guard.mjs.
